title: Add Dark Mode Toggle To Expo App

prompt.text: In my Expo app, add a dark mode toggle and make the dashboard cards use theme colors. Open a PR when it passes tests.

clarifyingQuestions.question: Should the app follow the system theme, use a manual toggle, or support both?
clarifyingQuestions.answer: Support both: default to system theme, then let the manual toggle override it.
clarifyingQuestions.reason: Theme behavior changes app state, settings UI, and tests.

approvedPlan.clarifiedGoal: Create a GitHub branch and PR that adds theme-aware dashboard cards plus a persisted dark mode override in the Expo app.
inputs: Theme behavior: system default plus manual override
steps: Clone the selected repository with scoped GitHub credentials.
Inspect the Expo app structure and existing styling patterns.
Add a theme state provider with system default and persisted manual override.
Add a settings toggle and update dashboard cards to read theme colors.
Run the repo test and typecheck commands.
Push a feature branch and open a PR with summary, files changed, and verification results.
assumptions: The requested change is limited to UI theme behavior, not full design-system replacement.
Existing tests can run in the worker without secrets.
The repository permits branch creation with the connected GitHub token.
risks: The repo may have missing local setup steps not documented in package scripts.
Snapshot tests may need updates if theme colors affect rendered output.

milestones.message: Cloned mira-campus/rainboard and detected an Expo workspace.
Added theme provider, settings toggle, and theme-aware dashboard card styles.
npm test and npm run typecheck passed locally.
Pushed feature/mobile-theme-toggle and opened a pull request.

result.summary: Opened a PR that adds a system-aware dark mode toggle, persists manual preference, and updates dashboard cards to use theme tokens.
followUpSuggestions: Wait for GitHub Actions before merging.
Ask Raincloud to add theme screenshots if reviewers request visual proof.
